assert.description: Raises an error if its argument is false or nil; otherwise returns all arguments.

error.description: Terminates the last protected function called and returns message as the error object.

getmetatable.description: Returns the metatable of the given object.

ipairs.description: Returns an iterator for array-like table traversal.

loadfile.description: Loads a file as a Lua chunk.

pairs.description: Returns an iterator for table traversal.

pcall.description: Calls a function in protected mode.

print.description: Receives any number of arguments and prints their values.

rawequal.description: Checks whether two values are equal without invoking metamethods.

rawget.description: Gets the real value of table[index] without invoking metamethods.

rawlen.description: Returns the length of an object without invoking metamethods.

rawset.description: Sets the real value of table[index] without invoking metamethods.

require.description: Loads a module by name, and returns it

select.description: Returns a subset of its arguments.

setmetatable.description: Sets the metatable for the given table.

tonumber.description: Converts its argument to a number.

tostring.description: Converts its argument to a string.

type.description: Returns the type of its only argument.

xpcall.description: Calls a function in protected mode with a message handler.

functions.isyieldable: Checks whether the running coroutine can yield.
functions.resume: Starts or continues execution of a coroutine.
functions.status: Returns the status of a coroutine.
functions.wrap: Creates a coroutine and returns a function that resumes it.
functions.yield: Suspends execution of the calling coroutine.

fields.cpath: Path used by require for searching C loaders.
fields.loaded: Table used by require to control loaded modules.
fields.path: Path used by require for searching Lua loaders.
fields.preload: Table to store loaders for specific modules.
fields.searchers: Table used by require to control how to load modules.
fields.searchpath: Searches for a file in a path.
fields.loadlib: Dynamically links the host program with a C library.

functions.byte: Returns internal numeric codes of characters.
functions.char: Receives zero or more integers and returns a string with corresponding characters.
functions.dump: Returns a binary representation of a function.
functions.find: Looks for the first match of pattern in the string.
functions.format: Returns a formatted version of its variable number of arguments.
functions.gmatch: Returns an iterator over pattern matches in a string.
functions.gsub: Returns a copy of the string with substitutions.
functions.len: Returns the length of a string.
functions.lower: Returns a copy of the string with uppercase letters changed to lowercase.
functions.match: Looks for the first match of pattern in the string.
functions.pack: Returns a binary string containing the values packed.
functions.packsize: Returns the size of a string resulting from pack.
functions.rep: Returns a string that is the concatenation of n copies of a string.
functions.reverse: Returns a string with bytes reversed.
functions.sub: Returns the substring of a string.
functions.unpack: Returns the values packed in a binary string.
functions.upper: Returns a copy of the string with lowercase letters changed to uppercase.

functions.char: Receives zero or more integers and returns a UTF-8 string.
functions.charpattern: Pattern matching exactly one UTF-8 byte sequence.
functions.codepoint: Returns the code points of characters in a string.
functions.codes: Returns an iterator over UTF-8 codes in a string.
functions.len: Returns the number of UTF-8 characters in a string.
functions.offset: Returns the position where encoding of n-th character starts.

functions.concat: Concatenates table elements into a string.
functions.insert: Inserts an element in a given position.
functions.move: Moves elements from one table to another.
functions.pack: Creates a table from all its arguments.
functions.remove: Removes an element from a given position.

functions.ceil: Returns the smallest integral value larger than or equal to x.
functions.deg: Converts angle from radians to degrees.
functions.floor: Returns the largest integral value smaller than or equal to x.
functions.huge: A value larger than any other numeric value.
functions.maxinteger: The maximum value for an integer.
functions.mininteger: The minimum value for an integer.
functions.modf: Returns the integral and fractional parts of a number.
functions.rad: Converts angle from degrees to radians.
functions.random: Returns a pseudo-random integer or float.
functions.randomseed: Sets the seed for pseudo-random generator.
functions.type: Returns whether a number is integer or float.

functions.flush: Saves any written data to file.
functions.input: Gets or sets default input file.
functions.lines: Iterates over lines of a file.
functions.output: Gets or sets default output file.
functions.popen: Starts a program in a separate process.
functions.tmpfile: Returns a handle for a temporary file.
functions.type: Checks whether object is a valid file handle.

functions.clock: Returns an approximation of CPU time used.
functions.difftime: Returns difference in seconds between times.
functions.exit: Calls the host program to exit.
functions.getenv: Returns value of an environment variable.
functions.tmpname: Returns a string with a file name that can be used for a temporary file.

functions.debug: Enters an interactive mode with user.
functions.getinfo: Returns a table with information about a function.
functions.getlocal: Returns name and value of a local variable.
functions.getupvalue: Returns name and value of an upvalue.
functions.getuservalue: Returns Lua value associated with userdata.
functions.setlocal: Assigns value to a local variable.
functions.setuservalue: Sets Lua value associated with userdata.
functions.traceback: Builds extended error message with traceback.
functions.upvalueid: Returns unique identifier for an upvalue.
functions.upvaluejoin: Makes two upvalues refer to the same value.
